@graph.rdfs:label: The Velvet Mask
@graph.sa:description: An intricate shadow-government controlling half of Blackwater's real economy through smuggling, theft, black-market trade, extortion, and corruption. The Velvet Mask operates with velvet gloves and steel underneath—their power rests not on crude violence but on leverage, secrets, and mutually profitable arrangements. To outsiders, the Mask seems invisible; to insiders, it is inescapable. The organization neither advertises nor recruits; you must be invited, and the Mask knows everything about you before you are invited. Their leader—known only as 'Vex'—has never been seen in person.

sa:role.rdfs:label: Street Asset
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Approached by Mask representative; proved reliability through completed small jobs; demonstrated discretion.
sa:description: Receive payments for passive intelligence (gossip, cargo movements, schedule variations)
Access to Mask safe houses and supply caches scattered throughout Blackwater
Introduction to Mask fence network; can offload stolen goods at fair value plus 10% finder's fee
Plausible deniability: the Mask takes fall for your crimes if caught working 'deniable' jobs
Protection from street-level predators; Mask will retaliate against muggings or exploitation

sa:role.rdfs:label: Specialist
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Years as Street Asset, demonstrated expertise (theft, forgery, assassination, or hacking), one major score for the Mask.
sa:description: Contracted directly for high-value jobs; earn 5,000–20,000 credits per assignment
Access to Mask quartermaster: lockpicks, forged documents, hacking tools, poisons, safe houses
Anonymity guaranteed: the Mask will misdirect authorities and rival crews hunting you
Right to freelance between contracts without Mask interference (so long as you don't expose Mask operations)
Can request 1d4 Street Assets for backup on approved jobs (minimal overhead paid by Mask)

sa:role.rdfs:label: Operative
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Specialist for five years, executed flawless high-stakes operations, proven judgment and loyalty.
sa:description: Earn 15,000–50,000 credits per contract, often with negotiated cut of scores
Authority to hire and direct smaller specialists for complex operations
Access to Mask's encrypted communication network; can request dossiers on marks/rivals
Protection expands: Mask's lawyers, enforcers, and corrupt officials can bail you out
Clearance to know the identity of 1–2 other Operatives (network building)
Can requisition equipment worth up to 25,000 credits per job

sa:role.rdfs:label: Mask Boss
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Operative for six years, kept a district profitable under pressure, and earned Vex's confidence as a steady hand rather than a loud one.
sa:description: Runs a local rackets network as a recognized boss; other crews answer when the Mask points at a street
Can command 1d6 Operatives or 2d6 Specialists for major jobs and collection runs
Settles disputes between crews, fixes payment problems, and keeps profitable areas from turning into open war
Access to Mask blackmail ledgers, protected routes, and trusted fences for moving large sums or sensitive cargo
Can approve deniable retaliation, forced buyouts, and targeted removals inside assigned territory
Earns regional profit share and one cut of every major score routed through the district
